channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: A Kremlin official says Russia sees efforts to end Ukraine war as a drawn-out process
item.description: Russia views efforts to end its three-year war with Ukraine as “a drawn-out process,” a Kremlin spokesman said Monday, after U.S. President Donald Trump expressed frustration with the two countries’ leaders as he tries to bring about a truce.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Greenland's prime minister says US will not 'get' island
item.description: Jens-Frederik Nielsen, Greenland's prime minister, said in a Facebook post on Sunday that the United States will not be able to annex the resource-rich island in the Atlantic.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says ‘terrorists who attacked the beautiful Turnberry’ have been captured
item.description: President Trump said he was informed that the “terrorists who attacked" his Turnberry golf resort in Scotland had been captured.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: French right-wing leader Marine Le Pen found guilty of embezzling public funds, barred from running for office
item.description: French right-wing leader Marine Le Pen is awaiting a sentence after a national court convicted her and other members of her National Rally party of embezzlement on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bangkok to review quake safety after collapse of high-rise leaves dozens missing
item.description: Thai authorities said they are investigating why a single office tower under construction in Bangkok collapsed during an earthquake Friday that otherwise caused limited damage in the capital.
item.dc:creator: JERRY HARMER, JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI and ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US armored vehicle missing in Lithuania pulled from swamp but fate of 4 soldiers is still unknown
item.description: VILNIUS, Lithuania (AP) — A U.S. armored vehicle that went missing in Lithuania has been retrieved from a swamp after a six-day search but there is still no information about the fate of the four American soldiers who were on board, Lithuanian officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: LIUDAS DAPKUS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli military orders evacuation of most of Gaza's southern city of Rafah
item.description: The Israeli military has issued sweeping evacuation orders covering most of Rafah, indicating it could soon launch another major ground operation in the southernmost city in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen is barred from seeking public office for embezzlement
item.description: A French court has barred Marine Le Pen from seeking public office for five years for embezzlement in a hammer blow to the far-right leader’s presidential hopes.
item.dc:creator: Sylvie Corbet, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Earthquake compounds humanitarian crisis in Myanmar, where death toll has passed 1,700
item.description: Aid groups and the U.N. are warning that a massive earthquake that rocked Myanmar could exacerbate hunger and disease outbreaks in a country already wracked by food shortages, mass displacement and civil war.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US airstrikes pound Yemen overnight, killing at least 3, Houthi rebels say
item.description: Suspected U.S. airstrikes have struck around Yemen’s rebel-held capital overnight into Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump: There's a 'psychological deadline' for Putin to agree to ceasefire
item.description: President Trump said late Sunday there is a “psychological deadline” for Russia to agree to a ceasefire in Ukraine, shortly after he had expressed frustration with Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Trump finds fault with both Putin and Zelenskyy as he tries to push for deal to end war in Ukraine
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump lashed out at both Vladimir Putin and Volodymyr Zelenskyy on Sunday, expressing frustration with the Russian and Ukrainian leaders as he struggles to forge a truce to end the war.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Chair of African charity Prince Harry co-founded says the royal orchestrated a bullying campaign
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The chairperson of an African charity co-founded by Prince Harry accused the royal on Sunday of orchestrating a bullying and harassment campaign to try to force her out as she pushed back following his abrupt resignation from the organization.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump 'very angry' at Putin's remarks on Zelensky
item.description: President Trump said he is “very angry" and "pissed off" at remarks Russian President Vladimir Putin made Friday about Ukraine President Volodymyr Zelensky, suggesting he is not a legitimate leader.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Trump threatens to bomb Iran unless they end nuclear weapons program and begin talks on new deal
item.description: Trump warned Iran after sending a letter to revive nuclear talks. With "maximum pressure" back on the table, tensions between the two nations continue to rise.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian attack on hospital, shopping center leaves 2 dead as Ukraine braces for fresh offensive
item.description: A Russian drone attack killed two people in Kharkiv on Sunday amid a fresh offensive ahead of peace talks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Officials survey damage in Myanmar's earthquake-devastated central areas
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Friday’s deadly earthquake rattled most of Myanmar and Thailand but certain areas sustained the heaviest damage, including flattened buildings and many lost lives. The death toll of the 7.7 magnitude earthquake on Friday rose quickly in Myanmar and was at 1,644 victims by Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Smell of death permeates Myanmar cities after quake kills over 1,600 and leaves countless buried
item.description: MANDALAY, Myanmar (AP) — The smell of decaying bodies permeated the streets of Myanmar's second-largest city on Sunday as people worked frantically by hand to clear rubble in the hope of finding someone still alive, two days after a massive earthquake struck that killed more than 1,600 people and le
item.dc:creator: THEIN ZAW, DAVID RISING and GRANT PECK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Medical supplies in great need as international assistance flows into Myanmar after earthquake
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Emergency aid has streamed into Southeast Asia in the two days since a massive earthquake struck Myanmar and Thailand. Relief efforts are focused on Myanmar, where the estimated death toll rose to 1,644 by Sunday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN P. D. HANNON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump golfs with Finland's president amid icy relationship with EU
item.description: President Trump joined the leader of Finland, a member of the European Union (EU), for a round of golf Saturday, despite trade tensions with the executive body, which recently announced levies on agricultural products and clothing items imported from the United States slated to take effect in mid-Ap
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Syria swears in new transitional government 4 months after Assad's removal
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Syria’s new transitional government was sworn in Saturday nearly four months after the Assad family was removed from power and as the new authorities in Damascus work to bring back stability to the war-torn country.
item.dc:creator: GHAITH ALSAYED and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Faye Hall, American detained by Taliban, has been released
item.description: Faye Hall, an American woman who had been detained by the Taliban since February, has been freed, a source with knowledge of the release told Fox News Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Countries sending humanitarian aid after Myanmar earthquake
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The death toll from Myanmar's powerful 7.7 magnitude earthquake keeps climbing amid rescue efforts. The military government said Saturday that 1,644 people have been killed, with thousands of others injured and dozens missing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Doctor cites the pope's 'surprising improvement' after surviving life-threatening crises
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis has shown ‘’a truly surprising improvement’’ since returning to the Vatican to convalesce after surviving a life-threatening bout with double-pneumonia, the doctor who coordinated the pontiff’s five-week hospitalization said Saturday. “I find him very lively,’’ Dr.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Death toll in Burma, Thailand earthquake rises to more than 1,600 victims
item.description: The death toll from the devastating earthquake that rattled Burma and Thailand on Friday has risen sharply to more than 1,650 victims. Nearly 140 people still missing, Burma's ruling military said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Signal chat shows isolationist Vance as Trump flexes control abroad
item.description: The war plans group chat uncovered by a journalist at The Atlantic exposed some daylight between Vice President Vance and others in the administration, showing Vance’s hesitance about launching a military strike that President Trump has hailed this week as necessary and successful.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Israel strikes Beirut for the first time since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war
item.description: Israel has launched an attack on Beirut, marking its first strike on Lebanon's capital since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war last November.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Denmark in Trump's crosshairs as Vance makes ambitious appeal to Greenland
item.description: Vice President Vance looked to appeal to Greenland by urging it to side with the US, not Russia or China, after it seeks independence from Denmark.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance slams Denmark's handling of Greenland security
item.description: Vice President Vance on Friday slammed Denmark as failing to prioritize Greenland’s security, escalating rhetoric between Washington and Copenhagen amid President Trump’s claims to take over the island.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Suspected US strikes pummel Houthi-controlled areas of Yemen
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes pummeled sites across Yemen controlled by the Houthi rebels early Friday, including neighborhoods in the capital, Sanaa.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney says 'old relationship' with US 'is over' amid tension over Trump tariffs
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney on Thursday remarked on his country's relationship with the United States amid tensions over trade agreements.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: JD Vance joining his wife for Greenland trip on Friday
item.description: The delegation is set to tour the northernmost security installation for the U.S. military, the Space Force's Pituffik Space Base.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: US holds separate talks with Russians after meeting Ukrainians to discuss a potential ceasefire
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — U.S. negotiators worked on a proposed partial ceasefire in the 3-year-old war in Ukraine on Monday, meeting representatives from Russia one day after holding separate talks with a team from Kyiv.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9
